# _docsmith_example.docsmith_example_plot { #ammonyte._docsmith_example.docsmith_example_plot }

```python
_docsmith_example.docsmith_example_plot(n=50)
```

Plot a damped sine wave, as a worked example of the docstring convention.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**n**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [50]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of points to sample.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tuple](`tuple`)\[[np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`), [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The ``(x, y)`` arrays that were plotted.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from ammonyte._docsmith_example import docsmith_example_plot

x, y = docsmith_example_plot()
plt.savefig('docs/reference/figures/docsmith_example_plot_example.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
```

![docsmith_example_plot example output](../figures/docsmith_example_plot_example.png)
